184.reason: [Duplicate assignment, but not an error; however, the BFS logic is flawed: the queue management uses 'count' and 'rear=front+count' which does not correctly track the queue tail, causing incorrect traversal order or missing vertices. The standard BFS should use a simple rear pointer incremented on each enqueue.]

87.reason: [When bool remains 0, last could be NULL if the new node's adjvex is greater than all existing nodes, causing a segmentation fault. Should check if last is not NULL before assigning last->next.]

209.reason: [The loop iterates over all vertices and starts BFS from every unvisited vertex. But problem requires traversal to always start from vertex 0 only. This causes incorrect output when graph has multiple components or after deletion if 0 is not first in loop. However, since graph is connected and 0 is always present, the real bug is that it may start from other vertices if 0 were skipped—but 0 is never skipped. Still, the specification says "start from vertex 0", so this loop is wrong. It should call BFS(0) directly, not loop through all i.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop iterates over all vertices and starts BFS from every unvisited vertex. But problem requires traversal to always start from vertex 0 only. This causes incorrect output when graph has multiple components or after deletion if 0 is not first in loop. However, since graph is connected and 0 is always present, the real bug is that it may start from other vertices if 0 were skipped—but 0 is never skipped. Still, the specification says "start from vertex 0", so this loop is wrong. It should call BFS(0) directly, not loop through all i.]
